description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a coarse, granular texture. Its surface displays a mix of gray, brown, and white mineral inclusions, with some areas appearing more weathered and others showing fresh, jagged breaks. The rock has a dense, solid appearance, with visible quartz or feldspar veins running through some sections, adding to its visual complexity. The coloration is mottled, with darker gray dominating the upper surfaces and lighter, almost creamy patches along the sides and base. There are no visible cavities or openings, and the rock appears to be a solid mass throughout. A small, faint marking or label is visible on one side, suggesting it may have been cataloged or moved by humans.
description: A dense, irregularly shaped rock with a coarse, mottled surface of gray, brown, and white mineral patches. Its rugged texture and solid form suggest durability and natural origin.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's angular top and a mix of gray and brown tones, with white mineral streaks visible on the left side.
description_view_1: The rock appears more blocky from this angle, with a prominent dark gray surface and a small, faint marking or label near the base.
description_view_2: This side shows a flatter profile with a blend of gray and brown, and more visible white mineral inclusions along the edge.
description_view_3: The base is more exposed here, revealing a rough, lighter-colored surface with prominent white mineral patches.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
